title: NaNoWriMo Calculator - Track Novel Writing Progress
tagline: Calculate daily word count targets, track your NaNoWriMo progress, and estimate completion dates for your 50,000-word novel challenge.
intro: Enter your word goal, dates, and current word count to get your daily writing target and estimated completion date.

about.heading: About the NaNoWriMo Calculator
about.body:
NaNoWriMo, short for National Novel Writing Month, is an annual creative writing challenge held every November. The goal is simple but demanding: write a 50,000-word novel in just 30 days. Launched in 1999 with only 21 participants, NaNoWriMo has grown into a global phenomenon with hundreds of thousands of writers pushing themselves to complete a draft novel every year. The challenge is beloved for removing perfectionism from the writing process — you write fast, you write messy, and you celebrate quantity over quality during those 30 days.

The core math behind NaNoWriMo is straightforward. A 50,000-word goal over 30 days requires exactly 1,667 words per day (rounding up from 1,666.67). Miss a day and that number climbs — fall two days behind and you owe yourself 3,334 extra words at the end. The NaNoWriMo Calculator takes the stress out of this arithmetic by computing exactly how many words you need to write each remaining day given your current count and deadline.

The calculator works for any writing challenge, not just the official November event. Camp NaNoWriMo events in April and July allow custom word goals from 10,000 to 1,000,000 words. NaNoWriMo Young Writers Program lets students set their own targets. This tool adapts to any start date, end date, and word goal — so whether you are targeting 80,000 words over 60 days or 10,000 words in a week, the daily target and progress metrics update automatically.

Pacing is critical to NaNoWriMo success. Writers who stay close to the daily average are statistically much more likely to finish than those who plan to catch up in marathon sessions at the end. The required daily target calculation in this calculator always reflects the current remaining gap divided by the remaining days, so you always know exactly what today's quota should be.

Using writing speed (words per hour), the calculator can also estimate how many hours of writing time you need each day. A typical prose writer produces between 250 and 1,000 words per hour depending on how much plotting and planning they have done in advance. Knowing your personal speed turns the abstract word count target into concrete time blocked on your calendar.

Whether you are a first-time NaNoWriMo participant or a seasoned veteran, the NaNoWriMo Progress Calculator helps you stay motivated, plan smarter, and cross the finish line with your draft intact. Bookmark this page and update your word count every writing session to keep your progress visible and your momentum high.

examples.heading: NaNoWriMo calculator examples
examples.intro: Four realistic NaNoWriMo scenarios showing progress, daily targets, and pacing needs.

items.note: Standard NaNoWriMo starting position. Write 1,667 words every day to finish exactly on time.
On track — written exactly as much as needed. Required daily target stays at 1,667 for the remaining 18 days.
Behind schedule by 6,670 words. Must write 2,000 words each of the 20 remaining days to catch up.
Custom Camp NaNoWriMo goal. Ahead of the 1,333 words/day average, so the required daily rate has dropped slightly.

howto.heading: How to use the NaNoWriMo calculator
steps: Enter your total word goal (50,000 for standard NaNoWriMo, or any custom target for Camp NaNoWriMo).
Set your start and end dates — November 1 and November 30 for the main event, or custom dates for Camp events.
Enter your current word count to reflect how far you have already written.
Click Calculate to see your required daily word target, progress percentage, words remaining, and estimated completion date.
Optionally enter your writing speed in words per hour to convert the daily word target into writing time needed per day.

items.q: What is the daily word count target for standard NaNoWriMo?
items.a: The standard NaNoWriMo target is 50,000 words in 30 days, which works out to 1,667 words per day (1,666.67 rounded up). Missing a single day raises your remaining daily quota by about 56 words, so consistent daily writing is strongly recommended.

items.q: What happens if I miss a few days?
items.a: The calculator automatically recalculates your required daily target based on your current word count and the remaining days. If you have fallen behind, the daily target will be higher than 1,667 — the calculator shows exactly how many extra words per day you need to write to catch up.

items.q: Can I use this calculator for Camp NaNoWriMo?
items.a: Yes. Camp NaNoWriMo events in April and July allow custom word goals and custom date ranges. Simply change the total word goal and adjust the start and end dates to match your Camp event. The calculator works identically for any goal and timeframe.

items.q: How accurate is the writing speed estimate?
items.a: Writing speed varies enormously depending on whether you are drafting from an outline, writing from scratch, or editing as you go. Most fiction writers draft between 250 and 750 words per hour. Entering your personal average gives a realistic picture of how much calendar time you need to block for writing each day.

items.q: Does word count include dialogue, notes, and outlines?
items.a: For the official NaNoWriMo event, only prose that is part of the novel counts toward the 50,000 words. Outlines, notes, and non-fiction writing do not count. For personal challenges tracked with this calculator, you can count whatever you decide — the calculator simply does the arithmetic.

items.q: What is a realistic writing speed for a first-time NaNoWriMo participant?
items.a: First-time participants often average 300–500 words per hour, meaning the 1,667-word daily target takes roughly 2–4 hours of writing time. Participants who outline their story in advance tend to write faster because they spend less time deciding what happens next. The optional writing speed field in this calculator helps you estimate your daily time commitment.
